reason: Adding the noexec option to /home is a filesystem mount option change that can prevent execution of binaries located in /home, potentially breaking applications or scripts that rely on executing files from that directory. According to the policy, any partitioning/filesystem change is considered too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential application breakage due to noexec; Loss of ability to run scripts or binaries from /home; Impact on user-installed software; Risk of lockout if critical binaries reside in /home
